source.title: The 'in's and out's' of descending pain modulation from the rostral ventromedial medulla

confidence_rationale: High-quality review (Trends in Neurosciences, Heinricher lab has canonical authority on RVM circuit). Mapping fidelity is moderate: the paper definitively characterises the PAG/RVM mechanism as behavioural-state gating via ON/OFF cells, NOT efference-copy comparator -- this weakens the hypothesis that PAG/RVM IS the nociceptive comparator substrate but does not undermine MECH-256 itself (behavioural attenuation of self-generated pain, shown by Lalouni 2020 in this corpus, is real). The paper supports the existence of a rich, regulated descending nociceptive control system, while implying the efference-copy mechanism operates at a different anatomical level (spinal dorsal horn / somatosensory cortex, via corticospinal collaterals).
failure_signatures: PAG/RVM ON/OFF cell firing is governed by behavioural state (arousal, stress, defensive motivation) and opioid tone, NOT by motor command prediction -- the system does not implement efference-copy cancellation of predicted nociception from a_actual.
If MECH-256 were implemented primarily in PAG/RVM, the clinical literature would predict that lesioning PAG/RVM abolishes self-generated pain attenuation; the paper does not support this specificity.

Molecular identification of RVM cell types produces conflicting behavioural results, indicating the system's modulatory function is more context-sensitive and less mechanistically clean than a forward-model comparator.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The RVM exerts descending control of pain via two functionally defined cell classes: OFF-cells (antinociceptive, pause at nocifensive reflex) and ON-cells (pronociceptive, burst at nocifensive reflex). Their activity is regulated by inputs from PAG (emotionally/motivationally driven), spinal cord (ascending nociceptive signals), and brainstem nuclei. Top-down control reflects behavioural state -- stress, opioid tone, anticipation -- not per-step motor command prediction.
mapping.ree_translation: The PAG/RVM implements CONTEXTUAL PRECISION-GATING of nociceptive gain, not the efference-copy forward-model comparator proposed in MECH-256. In REE terms: the PAG/RVM shifts the expected precision (reliability) of harm signals based on motivational state -- higher expected pain in hypervigilant states, lower in stress-analgesia states. This is conceptually distinct from the MECH-256 mechanism where residual = z_harm_s_observed - E2_harm_s(z_harm_s_{t-1}, a_actual) on a per-step basis. The PAG/RVM modulates the BASELINE GAIN rather than subtracting an action-conditional prediction.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The review does not directly test whether the nociceptive efference-copy comparator (MECH-256) exists at ALL in biology -- it simply shows that the PAG/RVM is not its substrate. The behavioural evidence (Lalouni 2020, already in this corpus for SD-029) that self-generated pain IS attenuated implies the comparator mechanism operates elsewhere -- most likely at spinal dorsal horn (corticospinal collateral corollary discharge to dorsal horn interneurons) or somatosensory cortex (S1/insula forward-model subtraction). MECH-256 generalisation to z_harm_s is supported behaviourally but the anatomical substrate is NOT the descending PAG/RVM pathway.
mapping.source_context: Primarily rodent in vivo electrophysiology and pharmacology, with corroborating evidence from human imaging. Canonical review from the Heinricher lab (Oregon Health & Science University), which has defined the ON/OFF cell framework for three decades.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality is high: Heinricher is the field's authority on RVM cell-type function. Mapping fidelity is moderate: the paper is directly about the nociceptive descending pathway, but the gap it leaves open (that the efference-copy comparator operates elsewhere, not that it doesn't exist) requires inference. Transfer risk is low: the paper includes human imaging corroboration.
